stimulus: Pueraria montana (kudzu) is an East Asian vine that has become invasive in the southeastern United States, where it smothers native trees and weakens soil stability. P. montana can be controlled with broad-spectrum herbicides, but that approach can also harm beneficial plants growing nearby. Recent research on deploying species-specific fungal pathogens to suppress invasive plants may offer a path toward exclusively targeting P. montana, consequently ______blank
stem: Which choice most logically completes the text?

answerOptions.content: enhancing the ecological benefits of P. montana in the southeastern United States.
making individual P. montana vines more susceptible to existing broad-spectrum herbicides.
enabling native trees to resist P. montana without external intervention.
reducing invasive P. montana populations without harming other organisms.

rationale: Choice D is best. The principle established is that broad-spectrum herbicides also damage harmless plants, but species-specific fungal pathogens would target only kudzu. By the connector "consequently," the predicted outcome is the reduction of kudzu without collateral harm. Choice A contradicts the text, which describes kudzu's effects only as harmful. Choice B misroutes the new approach back through herbicides. Choice C imagines an unstated mechanism (native-tree resistance) that is not part of the principle.
